revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[岡山県]][[総社市]]にある著名な古墳。前方後円墳で、古墳時代中期に作られた、全長360mの超巨大[[古墳]]で、[[岡山県]]では1位、全国では仁徳、応神、履中天皇陵につぐ4番目の大きさである。また上位3天皇陵をはじめ近畿の巨大[[古墳]]が宮内庁により内部への立ち入りが禁止されているのに対し、ここは立ち入り出来る古墳では最大のものであり、全国的に見ても貴重である。またこの大きさから古代吉備に大和に対抗できる強い王権があったことが想像できる。

[[岡山県]][[総社市]]にある著名な古墳。前方後円墳で、古墳時代中期に作られた、全長360mの超巨大[[古墳]]で、[[岡山県]]では1位、全国では仁徳、応神、履中天皇陵につぐ4番目の大きさである。また上位3天皇陵をはじめ近畿の巨大[[古墳]]が宮内庁により内部への立ち入りが禁止されているのに対し、ここは立ち入り出来る古墳では最大のものであり、全国的に見ても貴重である。またこの大きさから古代吉備に大和に対抗できる強い王権があったことが想像できる。 '''関連項目''' [[吉備路]]

1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。 2.岡山県総社市で造られた古墳時代中期の前方後円墳で全国４番目の規模を誇る。 '''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[岡山県]][[総社市]]にある著名な古墳。前方後円墳で、古墳時代中期に作られた、全長360mの超巨大[[古墳]]で、[[岡山県]]では1位、全国では仁徳、応神、履中天皇陵につぐ4番目の大きさである。また上位3天皇陵をはじめ近畿の巨大[[古墳]]が宮内庁により内部への立ち入りが禁止されているのに対し、ここは立ち入り出来る古墳では最大のものであり、全国的に見ても貴重である。またこの大きさから古代吉備に大和に対抗できる強い王権があったことが想像できる。

===関連項目=== * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[吉備路]]
[[category:古墳|つくりやまこふん]]

1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。
'''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。１号墳は一辺６０ｍの古墳時代前期のもので全国でも最大級の方墳である。昭和１１、１３年に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には三角縁神獣鏡、方格規矩四神鏡、方格規矩四神鏡、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。３号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で３８×３０ｍである。昭和４０年には竪穴式石室からは斜縁二神二獣鏡、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長５０mの前方後円墳（２号墳）や、小型古墳の４号墳がありいずれも６世紀前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。
---

1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。
'''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。１号墳は一辺６０ｍの古墳時代前期のもので全国でも最大級の方墳である。昭和１１、１３年に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には三角縁神獣鏡、方格規矩四神鏡、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。３号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で３８×３０ｍである。昭和４０年には竪穴式石室からは斜縁二神二獣鏡、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長５０mの前方後円墳（２号墳）や、小型古墳の４号墳があり、この二つは６世紀前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山１号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・素環頭大刀などが明治４４年に出土している。
---

1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。
'''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は古代出雲王陵の丘という名の公園となっている。１号墳は一辺６０ｍの古墳時代前期のもので全国でも最大級の方墳である。昭和１１、１３年に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には三角縁神獣鏡、方格規矩四神鏡、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。３号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で３８×３０ｍである。昭和４０年には竪穴式石室からは斜縁二神二獣鏡、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長５０mの前方後円墳（２号墳）や、小型古墳の４号墳があり、この二つは６世紀前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山１号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・素環頭大刀などが明治４４年に出土している。
---

revision.comment: 岡山・造山古墳所在地訂正
revision.text:
'''造山古墳'''（'''つくりやまこふん'''）は[[日本]]の[[古墳]]。 ---- 1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。 '''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は「古代出雲王陵の丘」という名の公園となっている。１号墳は一辺60mの古墳時代前期のもので全国でも最大級の方墳である。[[昭和]]11年、13年（[[1936年]]、[[1938年]]）に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には[[三角縁神獣鏡]]、[[方格規矩四神鏡]]、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。3号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で38×30mである。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）には竪穴式石室からは[[斜縁二神二獣鏡]]、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長50mの前方後円墳（2号墳）や、小型古墳の4号墳があり、この二つは[[6世紀]]前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山1号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・[[素環頭大刀]]などが[[明治]]44年（[[1911年]]）に出土している。 ---- 2.岡山県岡山市で造られた古墳時代中期の前方後円墳で全国4番目の規模を誇る。 '''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]にある著名な古墳。前方後円墳で、古墳時代中期に作られた、全長360mの超巨大古墳で、岡山県では1位、全国では[[仁徳天皇陵|仁徳]]、[[応神天皇陵|応神]]、[[履中天皇陵]]につぐ4番目の大きさである。また上位3天皇陵をはじめ[[近畿]]の巨大古墳が[[宮内庁]]により内部への立ち入りが禁止されているのに対し、ここは立ち入り出来る古墳では最大のものであり、全国的に見ても貴重である。またこの大きさから古代[[吉備]]に[[大和朝廷|大和]]に対抗できる強い王権があったことが想像できる。
また、[[総社市]]にも同音の[[作山古墳]]（つくりやまこふん）があり、地元では造山は「ぞうざん」、作山は「さくざん」と区別して呼んでいる。 ==関連項目== * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[吉備路]] [[category:古墳|つくりやまこふん]]

'''造山古墳'''（'''つくりやまこふん'''）は[[日本]]の[[古墳]]。 ---- 1.島根県安来市で造られた古墳時代前期の最大級の方墳。 '''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は「古代出雲王陵の丘」という名の公園となっている。１号墳は一辺60mの古墳時代前期のもので全国でも最大級の方墳である。[[昭和]]11年、13年（[[1936年]]、[[1938年]]）に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には[[三角縁神獣鏡]]、[[方格規矩四神鏡]]、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。3号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で38×30mである。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）には竪穴式石室からは[[斜縁二神二獣鏡]]、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長50mの前方後円墳（2号墳）や、小型古墳の4号墳があり、この二つは[[6世紀]]前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山1号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・[[素環頭大刀]]などが[[明治]]44年（[[1911年]]）に出土している。この素環頭大刀の復元を刀匠　河内国平が行ったがその現物は安来市立[[和鋼博物館]]に展示されている。 ---- 2.岡山県岡山市で造られた古墳時代中期の前方後円墳で全国4番目の規模を誇る。 '''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]にある著名な古墳。前方後円墳で、古墳時代中期に作られた、全長360mの超巨大古墳で、岡山県では1位、全国では[[仁徳天皇陵|仁徳]]、[[応神天皇陵|応神]]、[[履中天皇陵]]につぐ4番目の大きさである。また上位3天皇陵をはじめ[[近畿]]の巨大古墳が[[宮内庁]]により内部への立ち入りが禁止されているのに対し、ここは立ち入り出来る古墳では最大のものであり、全国的に見ても貴重である。またこの大きさから古代[[吉備]]に[[大和朝廷|大和]]に対抗できる強い王権があったことが想像できる。
また、[[総社市]]にも同音の[[作山古墳]]（つくりやまこふん）があり、地元では造山は「ぞうざん」、作山は「さくざん」と区別して呼んでいる。 ==関連項目== * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[吉備路]] [[category:古墳|つくりやまこふん]]

'''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')は[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は「古代出雲王陵の丘」という名の公園となっている。 １号墳は一辺60mの古墳時代前期のもので、この時代のものとしては全国でも最大級の方墳である。[[昭和]]11年、13年（[[1936年]]、[[1938年]]）に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には[[三角縁神獣鏡]]、[[方格規矩四神鏡]]、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、刀子などが発掘された。
3号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で38×30mである。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）には竪穴式石室からは[[斜縁二神二獣鏡]]、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長50mの前方後円墳（2号墳）や、小型古墳の4号墳があり、この二つは[[6世紀]]前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。
近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山1号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・[[素環頭大刀]]などが[[明治]]44年（[[1911年]]）に出土している。この素環頭大刀の復元を刀匠　河内国平が行ったがその現物は安来市立[[和鋼博物館]]に展示されている。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] [[category:古墳|つくりやまこふんしまね]]

'''造山古墳'''('''つくりやまこふん''')は[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は「古代出雲王陵の丘」という名の公園となっている。
==概要== 1号墳は一辺60mの[[古墳時代]]前期のもので、この時代のものとしては全国でも最大級の方墳である。[[昭和]]11年、13年（[[1936年]]、[[1938年]]）に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には[[三角縁神獣鏡]]、[[方格規矩四神鏡]]、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製管玉、鉄刀、鉄剣、[[刀子]]などが発掘された。
3号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で38×30mである。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）には竪穴式石室からは[[斜縁二神二獣鏡]]、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他兵陵最高所には全長50mの前方後円墳（2号墳）や、小型古墳の4号墳があり、この二つは[[6世紀]]前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。
近隣には、大成古墳もあり造山1号墳同様、古墳時代前期に築造、全国最大規模の方墳であり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・[[素環頭大刀]]などが[[明治]]44年（[[1911年]]）に出土している。この素環頭大刀の復元を刀匠 河内国平が行ったがその現物は安来市立[[和鋼博物館]]に展示されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]

'''造山古墳'''（つくりやまこふん）は[[島根県]][[安来市]]にある著名な古墳群。市が古墳群を整備し現在は「古代出雲王陵の丘」という名の公園となっている。
==概要== 1号墳は一辺60メートルの[[古墳時代]]前期のもので、この時代のものとしては全国でも最大級の方墳である。[[昭和]]11年、13年（[[1936年]]、[[1938年]]）に竪穴式石棺が次々と発見された。副葬品には[[三角縁神獣鏡]]、[[方格規矩四神鏡]]、紡錘車型石製品、ガラス製[[管玉]]、鉄刀、鉄剣、[[刀子]]などが発掘された。
3号墳も古墳時代前期に作られた方墳で、38×30メートルである。昭和40年（[[1965年]]）には竪穴式石室から[[斜縁二神二獣鏡]]、碧玉製管玉、ガラス小玉、刀子、ヤリガンナが出土している。この他、兵陵最高所には全長50メートルの前方後方墳（2号墳）や小型古墳（4号墳）があり、この2基は[[6世紀]]前半頃に築造されたものと考えられている。
近隣には、造山1号墳と同様に古墳時代前期に築造された方墳のうち全国最大規模である大成古墳もあり、石棺からは三角縁神獣鏡・[[素環頭大刀]]などが[[明治]]44年（[[1911年]]）に出土している。この素環頭大刀の復元が刀匠・河内国平によって行われ、その現物は安来市立[[和鋼博物館]]に展示されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:つくりやまこふん}} [[Category:安来市の歴史]] [[category:島根県の古墳]] [[Category:方墳]]

Together with other surrounding burial mounds, they are collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Burial Mounds''' (..."
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = {{ill|Arashima Burial Mounds|ja|荒島古墳群}}| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}
'''Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds''' (造山古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] burial mounds located in [[Yasugi]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], Japan. Together with other surrounding burial mounds, they are collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Burial Mounds''' (荒島古墳群)" as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Burial Mounds|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=note|This refers to the designation of the Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds as part of the "Arashima Subgroup" within the larger designation of the Arashima Burial Mounds<ref name=bunkaisan/>.}}. These burial mounds are also part of the broader [[Arashima Tomb Group]] and are preserved as the "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' Historical Park" by the city of Yasugi<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama Mound 1}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
### Mound 1 Mound 1 is a square burial mound from the early [[Kofun period]], measuring 60 meters on each side, making it one of the largest of its kind in Japan. [[Tate-ana-style burial chambers]] were discovered during excavations in [[1936]] and [[1938]]. Grave goods include [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]], [[rectangular mirrors with four gods]] (''Hōkaku Kikushishin Kyō''), spindle-shaped stone objects, glass [[tubular beads]], iron swords, iron daggers, and [[chisels]]. On December 16, 1936, Mound 1 was designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Mound 3 Mound 3, another square burial mound from the early Kofun period, measures 38 meters by 30 meters. Excavations in [[1965]] revealed grave goods such as [[two-deity two-beast mirrors]] (''Shaen Nishini Jūni Kyō''), jade tubular beads, glass beads, chisels, and adzes. It was designated as a historic site of Shimane Prefecture on May 31, 1965<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Other Mounds Other mounds in the area include: - **Mound 2**: A [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped burial mound) measuring 50 meters in total length, excavated in [[1991]] and dated to the early [[6th century]]. - **Mound 4**: A small burial mound, also excavated in [[1991]]. - **Mound 5**: Located separately from the main group.
In the vicinity is the Ōnari Kofun, a square burial mound from the early Kofun period and the largest of its kind in Japan, where [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]] and [[decorated ring-pommeled swords]] were discovered in [[1911]]. A replica of the ring-pommeled sword, crafted by master swordsmith Kawachi Kunihira, is displayed at the [[Wako Museum]] in Yasugi.
== Cultural Properties == Initially, Mound 1 and Mound 3 were designated separately as a national historic site and a prefectural historic site, respectively. On July 13, 1999, the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] consolidated the designations under the name "'''Arashima Burial Mounds'''," including Mounds 2–4, Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Tomb Group<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

### Other Mounds Other mounds in the area include: - '''Mound 2''': A [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped burial mound) measuring 50 meters in total length, excavated in [[1991]] and dated to the early [[6th century]]. - '''Mound 4''': A small burial mound, also excavated in [[1991]]. - '''Mound 5''': Located separately from the main group.
In the vicinity is the Ōnari Kofun, a square burial mound from the early Kofun period and the largest of its kind in Japan, where [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]] and [[decorated ring-pommeled swords]] were discovered in [[1911]]. A replica of the ring-pommeled sword, crafted by master swordsmith Kawachi Kunihira, is displayed at the [[Wako Museum]] in Yasugi.
== Cultural Properties == Initially, Mound 1 and Mound 3 were designated separately as a national historic site and a prefectural historic site, respectively. On July 13, 1999, the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] consolidated the designations under the name "'''Arashima Burial Mounds'''," including Mounds 2–4, Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Tomb Group<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

On July 13, 1999, the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] consolidated the designations under the name "'''Arashima Burial Mounds'''," including Mounds 2–4, Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Tomb Group<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = {{ill|Arashima Burial Mounds|ja|荒島古墳群}}| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}
'''Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds''' (造山古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] burial mounds located in [[Yasugi]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], Japan. Together with other surrounding burial mounds, they are collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Burial Mounds''' (荒島古墳群)" as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Burial Mounds|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=note|This refers to the designation of the Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds as part of the "Arashima Subgroup" within the larger designation of the Arashima Burial Mounds<ref name=bunkaisan/>.}}. These burial mounds are also part of the broader [[Arashima Tomb Group]] and are preserved as the "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' Historical Park" by the city of Yasugi<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama Mound 1}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
### Mound 1 Mound 1 is a square burial mound from the early [[Kofun period]], measuring 60 meters on each side, making it one of the largest of its kind in Japan. [[Tate-ana-style burial chambers]] were discovered during excavations in [[1936]] and [[1938]]. Grave goods include [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]], [[rectangular mirrors with four gods]] (''Hōkaku Kikushishin Kyō''), spindle-shaped stone objects, glass [[tubular beads]], iron swords, iron daggers, and [[chisels]]. On December 16, 1936, Mound 1 was designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Mound 3 Mound 3, another square burial mound from the early Kofun period, measures 38 meters by 30 meters. Excavations in [[1965]] revealed grave goods such as [[two-deity two-beast mirrors]] (''Shaen Nishini Jūni Kyō''), jade tubular beads, glass beads, chisels, and adzes. It was designated as a historic site of Shimane Prefecture on May 31, 1965<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Other Mounds Other mounds in the area include: - '''Mound 2''': A [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped burial mound) measuring 50 meters in total length, excavated in [[1991]] and dated to the early [[6th century]]. - '''Mound 4''': A small burial mound, also excavated in [[1991]]. - '''Mound 5''': Located separately from the main group.
In the vicinity is the Ōnari Kofun, a square burial mound from the early Kofun period and the largest of its kind in Japan, where [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]] and [[decorated ring-pommeled swords]] were discovered in [[1911]]. A replica of the ring-pommeled sword, crafted by master swordsmith Kawachi Kunihira, is displayed at the [[Wako Museum]] in Yasugi.
== Cultural Properties == Initially, Mound 1 and Mound 3 were designated separately as a national historic site and a prefectural historic site, respectively. On July 13, 1999, the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] consolidated the designations under the name "'''Arashima Burial Mounds'''," including Mounds 2–4, Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Tomb Group<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = {{ill|Arashima Burial Mounds|ja|荒島古墳群}}| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}
'''Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds''' (造山古墳群) are a group of [[Kofun period]] burial mounds located in [[Yasugi]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], Japan. Together with other surrounding burial mounds, they are collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Burial Mounds''' (荒島古墳群)" as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Burial Mounds|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=note|This refers to the designation of the Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds as part of the "Arashima Subgroup" within the larger designation of the Arashima Burial Mounds<ref name=bunkaisan/>.}}. These burial mounds are also part of the broader [[Arashima Tomb Group]] and are preserved as the "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' Historical Park" by the city of Yasugi<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education Bureau Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|publisher=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.
== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama Mound 1}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
### Mound 1 Mound 1 is a square burial mound from the early [[Kofun period]], measuring 60 meters on each side, making it one of the largest of its kind in Japan. [[Tate-ana-style burial chambers]] were discovered during excavations in [[1936]] and [[1938]]. Grave goods include [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]], [[rectangular mirrors with four gods]] (''Hōkaku Kikushishin Kyō''), spindle-shaped stone objects, glass [[tubular beads]], iron swords, iron daggers, and [[chisels]]. On December 16, 1936, Mound 1 was designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Mound 3 Mound 3, another square burial mound from the early Kofun period, measures 38 meters by 30 meters. Excavations in [[1965]] revealed grave goods such as [[two-deity two-beast mirrors]] (''Shaen Nishini Jūni Kyō''), jade tubular beads, glass beads, chisels, and adzes. It was designated as a historic site of Shimane Prefecture on May 31, 1965<ref>{{Cite book|last=Nagami|first=Hidetaka|last2=Miyake|first2=Hiroshi|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Burial Mounds in Yasugi City|year=1992|pages=1}}</ref>.
### Other Mounds Other mounds in the area include: - '''Mound 2''': A [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (keyhole-shaped burial mound) measuring 50 meters in total length, excavated in [[1991]] and dated to the early [[6th century]]. - '''Mound 4''': A small burial mound, also excavated in [[1991]]. - '''Mound 5''': Located separately from the main group.
In the vicinity is the Ōnari Kofun, a square burial mound from the early Kofun period and the largest of its kind in Japan, where [[Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors]] and [[decorated ring-pommeled swords]] were discovered in [[1911]]. A replica of the ring-pommeled sword, crafted by master swordsmith Kawachi Kunihira, is displayed at the [[Wako Museum]] in Yasugi.
== Cultural Properties == Initially, Mound 1 and Mound 3 were designated separately as a national historic site and a prefectural historic site, respectively. On July 13, 1999, the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] consolidated the designations under the name "'''Arashima Burial Mounds'''," including Mounds 2–4, Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Tomb Group<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:造山古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|qid=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|qq=|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|qq=|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|qq=|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:造山古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>
