==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. ==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:造山古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751}}; {{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42969444444444|N|133.19977777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11639744]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = Tsukuriyama Kofun Group| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群) is an {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. Together with other nearby Kofun groups, it has been collectively designated as the "'''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群)" and recognized as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by the Japanese government.<ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties [Historic Sites]|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>{{Refnest|group=Note|Tsukuriyama is categorized as a '''subgroup of the Arashima Kofun Group'''.<ref name=bunkaisan/>}} It is also part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Kofuns|qq=}}. The Yasugi City government has developed the area into a historical park named "'''Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill'''".<ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Education and Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Shimane Historic Sites: Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Mausoleum Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==

[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa from Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun}}{{small|Exhibited at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]]
'''Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun''' is a {{ill|square-shaped Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 60 meters on each side, one of the largest square mounds from the early Kofun period nationwide. Excavations in [[:en:Shōwa era|1936]] (Shōwa 11) and 1938 (Shōwa 13) revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Buried artifacts include {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, [[:en:TLV mirror|square-rim deity-and-animal mirrors]], spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass {{ill|Kudatama|lt=Kudatama|lt=Tubular beads|ja|管玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tubular beads|qq=}}, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Ko-dachi|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ko-dachi|qq=}}. On December 16, 1936, it was designated a National Historic Site under the name "Tsukuriyama Kofun."{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

'''Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun''' is a square mound dating to the early Kofun period, measuring 38 meters in length and 30 meters in width. Excavations in [[:en:1965|1965]] uncovered a pit-style stone chamber yielding a {{ill|two-deity two-beast mirror|ja|斜縁神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/two-deity two-beast mirror|qq=}}, jasper tubular beads, glass beads, a ko-dachi, and a yari-ganna. It was designated a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site on May 31, 1965.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992|p=1}}

On the highest point of the hill, there is '''Tsukuriyama 2 Kofun''', a {{ill|keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measuring 50 meters in length, and the smaller '''Tsukuriyama 4 Kofun'''. Both were excavated in 1991, revealing construction dating to the early 6th century.{{Sfn|Nagami|Miyake|1992}} A fifth mound, '''Tsukuriyama 5 Kofun''', is located slightly apart from the main group.

Nearby, the '''Ōnari Kofun''', another square mound from the early Kofun period, is the largest of its kind nationally. Excavations in [[:en:1911|1911]] revealed artifacts such as triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors and a {{ill|sokantō|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sokantō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A reproduction of the sokantō, crafted by master swordsmith {{ill|Kawachi Kunihira|ja|河内国平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Kunihira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内國平|1=Kunihira Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553389}}, is exhibited at the {{ill|Yasugi City Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasugi City Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
Initially, Tsukuriyama 1 Kofun was designated as a National Historic Site, and Tsukuriyama 3 Kofun was designated as a Shimane Prefecture Historic Site. In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs expanded the designation to include Tsukuriyama Kofun Group (2-4 Kofun), Ōnari Kofun, and the Shiozuyama Burial Group (1-11 Kofun), officially renaming them the "'''Arashima Kofun Group'''".<ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>

==Merged second translation==
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|position=top|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|width=250|caption={{left|Location.}}}}

'''Tsukuriyama Kofun Group''' (造山古墳群, Tsukuriyama Kofungun) is a collection of [[Kofun period]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi, Shimane]], Japan. Together with other nearby burial sites, it is designated as a National Historic Site under the name '''Arashima Kofun Group''' (荒島古墳群) <ref name=shimanebunkazai>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/kunisiseki.html|title=List of National Cultural Properties (Historic Sites)|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=bunkaisan>{{Cite web|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172875|title=Arashima Kofun Group|work=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>. The group is also part of the broader archaeological concept of the '''Arashima Burial Site Complex''' (荒島墳墓群). The city of Yasugi has developed the area into a historical park known as the '''Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill''' (古代出雲王陵の丘) <ref name=shimaneOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties Division|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shiseki/shisekidesu/shiseki01.html|title=Historic Sites in Shimane: Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Shimane Prefecture|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref><ref name=yasukiOuryounooka>{{Cite web|author=Yasugi City Tourism Association|url=https://yasugi-kankou.com/spot/spot-482/|title=Ancient Izumo Royal Tomb Hill|work=Yasugi City Tourism Guide|accessdate=2023-08-27}}</ref>.

== Overview ==
[[File:造山1号墳出土 円筒埴輪 (J-34492).JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|Haniwa excavated from Mound 1}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.}}]]
The 1st mound is a square-shaped Kofun (方墳) measuring 60 meters per side, one of the largest of its type from the early Kofun period. In 1936 and 1938, {{ill|vertical shaft-style burial chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical shaft-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were discovered, yielding grave goods such as {{ill|triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, square-rimmed mirrors, spindle-shaped stone artifacts, glass beads, iron swords, and {{ill|Kozuka|lt=Kozuka|lt=Tōsu (tool)|ja|刀子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsu (tool)|qq=}}. The site was designated a National Historic Site as "Tsukuriyama Kofun" on December 16, 1936 <ref name="永見三宅1992">{{Cite book|author=Ei Nagami, Hakase Miyake|title=Excavation Report of Tsukuriyama Kofun Group in Yasugi City|year=1992|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/2717|doi=10.24484/sitereports.2717|ref=harv}}</ref>.

The 3rd mound, another early Kofun period square-shaped mound, measures 38 by 30 meters. In 1965, a vertical shaft-style burial chamber yielded a {{ill|mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja|斜縁二神二獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror with slanted divine beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, jasper beads, glass beads, a Tōsu, and a drawknife. It was designated a Shimane Prefectural Historic Site in 1965 <ref name="永見三宅1992" />.

Other notable mounds in the group include:
* '''2nd mound''': A 50-meter-long {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dating to the early 6th century.
* '''4th mound''': A smaller Kofun from the same period.
* '''5th mound''': Located slightly away from the main group.

Nearby, the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|大成古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|qq=}}, also from the early Kofun period, is the largest square-shaped Kofun in Japan. Artifacts such as triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors and {{ill|Suwanto-tachi|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwanto-tachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were unearthed there in 1911. A replica of the Suwanto-tachi is displayed at the {{ill|Wako Museum|ja|和鋼博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wako Museum|1=Wakō Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418233}} in Yasugi City.

== Cultural Properties ==
In 1999, the Agency for Cultural Affairs consolidated the 1st through 4th mounds of Tsukuriyama, along with the Ōnari Kofun and the {{ill|Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja|塩津山墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuyama Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, into the National Historic Site designation for the Arashima Kofun Group <ref name=shimanebunkazai/><ref name=bunkaisan/>.
